name: Classic Challa
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in warm water in a large bowl.
2. When dissolved, add sugar, salt, and half of the flour.
3. Mix well. Add eggs and oil, then slowly stir in most of the remaining flour.
4. The dough will be quite heavy; add a little water and beat until the dough starts to pull away from the sides of the bowl.
5. Turn out onto a well-floured surface and knead for about 10 minutes. Add only enough flour to keep the dough manageable, kneading until it is smooth and elastic.
6. Place the dough in a large oiled bowl, turning so the top gets oiled as well. Cover with a damp towel and let rise in a warm place for about 2 hours, punching down every 20 minutes.
7. Divide the dough into three parts. Shape into loaves (or twist) and place in well-greased loaf pans or baking sheets.
8. Let rise until doubled in bulk. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
9. Brush the tops of the loaves with beaten egg and sprinkle with seeds.
10. Bake for 30 to 45 minutes, or until brown. Remove from the oven and cool on a rack.
